source.title: Hippocampal place-cell sequences depict future paths to remembered goals.

confidence_rationale: Rat hippocampal single-unit recordings showing that before goal-directed navigation in an open arena, the hippocampus generates brief place-cell sequences encoding spatial trajectories from the animal's current location toward a known goal. The sequences predict immediate future behaviour even for novel start-goal combinations. This is direct evidence that hippocampal trajectory proposals are conditioned on current goal / task state, not just on spatial position. That is exactly what MECH-267 claims: proposal_trajectories should be a function of operating mode and world state, not world state alone. Confidence 0.82 because this is a canonical result in rodent single-unit data, directly testing the claim that hippocampal proposals are state-and-goal-conditioned.
failure_signatures: The study shows goal-biased proposals during one kind of task (open-arena navigation). It does not dissociate whether the bias reflects the operating mode (external_task vs internal_planning vs replay) or just the current goal representation. MECH-267 specifically claims mode-conditioning; Pfeiffer & Foster show goal-conditioning, which is one instance of state-conditioning that in REE collapses to external_task mode.

Open-arena rat navigation is a specific behavioural context; generalising to the four REE operating modes (external_task, internal_planning, internal_replay, offline_consolidation) requires additional evidence from other contexts.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Before goal-directed navigation in an open arena, the rat hippocampus generates brief place-cell sequences that depict trajectories from the current location toward a known goal location. These sequences predict immediate future behaviour, including for novel start-goal combinations. This supports the interpretation that hippocampal sequence events (replay) also implement a goal-directed, trajectory-finding mechanism at specific times when memory retrieval is required.
mapping.ree_translation: MECH-267 claims that HippocampalModule.propose_trajectories should condition on operating_mode in addition to world_state. Pfeiffer & Foster demonstrate that hippocampal trajectory proposals are conditioned on current goal (a task-relevant variable), not only world state. This directly supports the architectural signature required by MECH-267: trajectory proposal content is task-dependent. In the REE mode taxonomy, Pfeiffer & Foster's paradigm is an external_task-mode-like context (active goal-directed navigation), and they show proposals biased toward goal-consistent trajectories. Extrapolating to the other modes (internal_planning -> counterfactual proposals; internal_replay -> past-successful proposals; offline_consolidation -> stability-check proposals) is a valid next step supported by other work in the field.
mapping.mapping_caveat: This paper specifically shows goal-conditioning, not operating-mode conditioning. In REE's taxonomy, 'external_task with a goal' and 'internal_planning with a goal' may both be present; Pfeiffer & Foster do not dissociate these. The paper is strong on the phenomenon but does not uniquely support the mode-taxonomy structure MECH-267 uses.
mapping.source_context: Rat (Long-Evans) single-unit recordings in an open arena with learned goal locations. Multiple goal positions, awake behaviour. Widely cited in the replay / prospective-coding literature.

confidence_components.notes: Very high source quality (Nature, canonical result). Mapping fidelity is good because the phenomenon -- goal-conditioned hippocampal trajectory proposals -- is exactly what MECH-267 requires at the first-mode-conditioning level. Transfer risk is moderate: rodent spatial navigation to REE's more abstract trajectory proposals in a gridworld carries some abstraction cost.
